statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of two lines. The first line is the size of the array, n. The second line is a string of binary digits representing the array.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return true if the array contains a subarray of ones where the last one is followed by zeros, otherwise return false.
statement.notes: The array contains at most 10000 elements.

hints.text: Use a sliding window approach to check for the subarray

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to start a sliding window and check if the last one in the window is followed by zero
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution would involve checking all possible subarrays
editorial.optimalSolution: Start the window from the end of the string, move it forward as long as the last one is followed by zero
commonMistakes: Not checking for the last character of the subarray
Not moving the window forward when a 0 is seen
